paper_evidence.summary: R3Mab shows efficacy against t(4;14)-positive multiple myeloma cells with FGFR3 overexpression through dual mechanisms: FGFR3 signaling inhibition and ADCC. Complete tumor growth inhibition in OPM2 and KMS11 xenografts.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Nearly complete abolishment of xenograft tumor growth

alignment.notes: Strong alignment between paper, GT and AI for this core finding

paper_evidence.summary: R3Mab shows efficacy against bladder cancer cells expressing FGFR3 S249C mutation through inhibition of constitutive receptor dimerization and signaling. Demonstrated in UMUC-14 cells and xenografts.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: 55% reduction in colonies >120μm; significant xenograft growth inhibition

alignment.notes: Important evidence missing from GT but correctly captured by AI

ai_improvements_over_gt.paper_support: Figure 5C-E, Figure 7D-E showing efficacy against S249C mutant bladder cancer
Figure 1A-D showing oncogenic role through knockdown studies

overall_judgement.summary: The AI extraction shows high accuracy in capturing both predictive and oncogenic evidence from this paper, with several improvements over the ground truth. It correctly identified key findings about R3Mab efficacy against both bladder cancer and multiple myeloma, including specific FGFR3 mutations and mechanisms of action. The extraction maintained high precision while achieving better recall than the ground truth.
strengths: Comprehensive capture of FGFR3 mutation-specific evidence
Accurate representation of dual mechanism of action (signaling inhibition and ADCC)
Identification of important oncogenic evidence missing from ground truth
weaknesses: Some redundancy in multiple myeloma evidence items that could be consolidated
Variant nomenclature not fully standardized across items
